title: How to use Mercury's Treasury product to manage a startup's runway

interlinking_suggestions.title: Mercury vs. RelayFi: Which is the Best US Bank Account for a Non-Resident LLC?
A Freelancer's Guide to the US-UK Tax Treaty

author.name: Samuel Chen
author.bio: A former product manager at a major fintech company, Samuel has deep expertise in the global payments landscape. He analyzes financial tools and strategies to help freelancers maximize their earnings and minimize fees.

reviewedBy.name: Dr. Alistair Finch
reviewedBy.bio: With a Ph.D. in Economics and over 15 years at a Big Four accounting firm, Alistair specializes in demystifying cross-border tax law for independent professionals. He focuses on risk mitigation and long-term financial planning.

key_takeaways: Adopt a 3-Tier Capital Framework to manage your funds for liquidity, mid-term growth, and long-term wealth, shifting your mindset from high-earner to strategic CFO.
Prioritize financial stability by first creating a 'Compliance Vault' holding 6 months of operating expenses plus 35% of your annual gross revenue in an FDIC-insured account.
For safe mid-term yield on excess cash, use U.S. Treasury Bills, but be aware that holding them in foreign accounts can trigger FBAR and Form 8938 reporting requirements.
Leverage your self-employed status to build long-term wealth by maximizing contributions to tax-advantaged retirement accounts like a SEP IRA or Solo 401(k).
summary: Global solo professionals often face financial anxiety and risk by managing their capital without a formal strategy, finding corporate tools like Mercury Treasury ill-suited for their needs. The core advice is to adopt a "3-Tier Capital Framework," segmenting funds into a high-yield savings account for liquidity, U.S. T-bills for safe mid-term growth, and tax-advantaged retirement accounts for long-term wealth. Implementing this system transforms financial complexity from a source of stress into a source of strength, giving you the control and confidence to operate like the strategic CFO of your own business.
ai_overview_answer: A smarter alternative to Mercury Treasury for a solo global professional is not a single product, but a strategic "Business-of-One" treasury framework. This approach organizes capital into three distinct layers to ensure stability, growth, and compliance. The first layer, for liquidity, uses an FDIC-insured high-yield savings account to hold six months of operating expenses plus a 35% tax reserve. For mid-term growth, excess cash is allocated to U.S. Treasury Bills, purchased directly via TreasuryDirect.gov to earn a safe yield. It's crucial to note that T-bill interest is subject to federal tax but exempt from state and local taxes, and expats must be mindful of FBAR reporting. The third layer focuses on long-term wealth, utilizing tax-advantaged retirement accounts like a SEP IRA or Solo 401(k) invested in low-cost index funds. This structured framework provides a comprehensive system for managing finances with the discipline of a CFO.
disclaimer: The information provided in this article is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal or financial advice. You should consult with a qualified professional for advice tailored to your individual situation.

itemListElement.name: How to use Mercury's Treasury product to manage a startup's runway

@graph.headline: How to use Mercury's Treasury product to manage a startup's runway
@graph.description: Global solo professionals often face financial anxiety and risk by managing their capital without a formal strategy, finding corporate tools like Mercury Treasury ill-suited for their needs. The core advice is to adopt a "3-Tier Capital Framework," segmenting funds into a high-yield savings account for liquidity, U.S. T-bills for safe mid-term growth, and tax-advantaged retirement accounts for long-term wealth. Implementing this system transforms financial complexity from a source of stress into a source of strength, giving you the control and confidence to operate like the strategic CFO of your own business.

step.name: Tier 1: The Bulletproof Liquidity Layer for Ultimate Peace of Mind
step.text: Commanding your finances begins not with a bold investment, but with a disciplined act of fortification. Before you can strategically grow your capital, you must build an untouchable fortress around the funds you absolutely cannot afford to lose. This is the sole purpose of Tier 1: capital preservation and absolute accessibility. Your first step is to calculate the precise amount this fortress must hold, your 'Compliance Vault,' using the formula: (6 months of total operating expenses) + (35% of your last 12 months' gross revenue). The right tool for this is a high-yield savings account (HYSA) at an FDIC-insured bank, which protects your cash deposits up to $250,000.

step.name: Tier 2: The Mid-Term Growth Engine (The Real Answer to Your Search)
step.text: With your Tier 1 vault funded, use excess cash you won't need for 6-24 months to earn a meaningful yield without market risk. The primary tool is the U.S. Treasury Bill (T-Bill), a short-term debt security issued by the U.S. government, which you can purchase directly at TreasuryDirect.gov. Be mindful of tax implications: interest is subject to federal income tax but exempt from state and local taxes. U.S. expats must also be aware of FBAR and Form 8938 reporting requirements if T-bills are held in foreign accounts.

step.name: Tier 3: The Long-Term Wealth Layer for Building Your Empire
step.text: For capital you can commit for at least five years, focus on tax-advantaged growth to build long-term wealth. As a self-employed professional, leverage high-powered retirement accounts like a SEP IRA or Solo 401(k), which have substantial contribution limits. The strategy is to automate contributions into a diversified portfolio of low-cost index funds or ETFs. This disciplined, long-term approach allows you to weather market volatility with confidence, knowing your short- and mid-term funds are secure.

mainEntity.name: What is the best alternative to Mercury Treasury for a solopreneur?

acceptedAnswer.text: The best alternative isn't a single product, but the strategic 3-Tier Capital Framework. This involves using an FDIC-insured high-yield savings account for liquidity (Tier 1), U.S. Treasury Bills via TreasuryDirect.gov for mid-term growth (Tier 2), and tax-advantaged retirement accounts like a SEP IRA for long-term wealth (Tier 3).

mainEntity.name: How can a solopreneur earn yield on business cash safely?

acceptedAnswer.text: For ultimate safety on operational cash, use an FDIC-insured high-yield savings account. For very low-risk yield on funds you won't need for 6-24 months, U.S. Treasury Bills (T-bills) are an excellent choice as they are backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. government.

mainEntity.name: Is the interest from a treasury account taxable for a US expat?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Interest income from U.S. Treasury securities is subject to U.S. federal income tax, and you must report it on your federal tax return. However, this income is exempt from all state and local income taxes.

mainEntity.name: What is the real difference between SIPC and FDIC insurance?

acceptedAnswer.text: FDIC (Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation) protects cash deposits (checking, savings) in an insured bank up to $250,000 in case of bank failure. SIPC (Securities Investor Protection Corporation) protects securities (stocks, bonds) and cash in a brokerage account up to $500,000 (including a $250,000 cash limit) in case of brokerage firm failure. SIPC does not protect against market losses.

mainEntity.name: Can I buy US Treasury Bills directly as an individual?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, you can purchase U.S. Treasury Bills directly from the government through the TreasuryDirect website. This allows you to bypass brokerage fees. You will need a Social Security or Taxpayer Identification Number, a U.S. address, and a U.S. bank account.

mainEntity.name: What is the minimum balance required for Mercury Treasury?

acceptedAnswer.text: Mercury Treasury currently requires a minimum balance of $250,000, making it a solution designed for well-funded companies rather than most solo professionals.

body.content: The "Business-of-One" Treasury: A Smarter Framework Than Mercury for Global Professionals

That instinct to make your capital work harder is what separates a professional from an amateur. But a corporate tool like **Mercury Treasury** is designed to manage a venture-backed startup’s runway, not the nuanced reality of a solo global professional. You don't need their product; you need their mindset. It’s time to shift from thinking like a high earner to operating as the strategic CFO of "Me, Inc."

As your business scales internationally, a simple bank account becomes a liability. It leaves you exposed to tax surprises, market volatility, and critical compliance traps. This is the source of "compliance anxiety"—the rational fear of mismanaging your success. You worry about accurately forecasting taxes, you hear daunting acronyms like FBAR (Report of Foreign Bank and Financial Accounts), and you wonder how investment income might complicate your Foreign Earned Income Exclusion (FEIE) status.

These are not vague fears; they are concrete operational risks. A formal treasury strategy is the antidote. It imposes a structure on your capital, transforming ambiguity into a clear, manageable system that gives you absolute control. The blueprint for this system is the **3-Tier Capital Framework**, a method for assigning every dollar a specific job. We will organize your capital into three distinct layers:

items: **The Liquidity Layer:** Your operational core and compliance shield, built for immediate accessibility and absolute safety.
**The Mid-Term Growth Layer:** Your intelligent yield engine, designed to make excess cash work for you without exposure to market volatility.
**The Long-Term Wealth Layer:** Your empire-building fund, focused on tax-advantaged growth to secure your future autonomy.

body.content: This framework is your new operating manual. It provides the structure to make rational, confident decisions, turning financial complexity from a source of stress into a source of strength.
Tier 1: The Bulletproof Liquidity Layer for Ultimate Peace of Mind

Commanding your finances begins not with a bold investment, but with a disciplined act of fortification. Before you can strategically grow your capital, you must build an untouchable fortress around the funds you absolutely cannot afford to lose. This is the sole purpose of Tier 1: **capital preservation and absolute accessibility**. This layer isn't your growth engine; it is your operational core, your compliance shield, and the bedrock of your financial peace of mind.
Your first step is to calculate the precise amount this fortress must hold. We call this your **"Compliance Vault,"** and funding it is your highest financial priority.

Calculate your number with this simple formula:
`(6 months of total operating expenses) + (35% of your last 12 months' gross revenue)`

The first part of the equation creates a robust six-month buffer to cover all your business and personal costs, insulating you from income volatility. The second part establishes a conservative tax fund, preventing the year-end surprise that creates so much anxiety. This total figure is your Tier 1 target.

The right tool for this job is the simplest and most secure: a **high-yield savings account (HYSA)** at a reputable, FDIC-insured bank. The yield is a secondary benefit; the primary feature is the protection offered by the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC), which insures your cash deposits up to $250,000. This is fundamentally different from SIPC insurance, which protects securities in a brokerage account. For your Tier 1 safety net, you need the certainty of FDIC coverage for your cash.

Ultimately, the stability of this entire framework relies on predictable cash flow. A bulletproof, compliant invoicing system is the engine that keeps your Tier 1 vault replenished, giving you the clarity and confidence to allocate new capital to your growth and wealth layers.
Tier 2: The Mid-Term Growth Engine (The Real Answer to Your Search)

With your Tier 1 vault fully funded, you’ve earned the right to ask the next critical question: How can I make my excess cash work harder without gambling on the market? This brings us to Tier 2, the true objective for most professionals searching for a **Mercury Treasury** alternative. This is the capital exceeding your six-month operational buffer and tax reserves—funds you don't anticipate needing for the next 6 to 24 months. The goal is powerful: **earn a meaningful yield that significantly outperforms a savings account without exposing your principal to market risk.**

Your primary tool for this mission is the U.S. Treasury Bill (T-Bill). T-bills are short-term debt securities issued by the U.S. government and are considered one of the safest investments in the world. For the self-directed professional, the advantage is clear: you can purchase them directly from the source at TreasuryDirect.gov, ensuring you receive the full return without paying advisory fees.

However, this is where professionals often stumble. The focus on yield can obscure the critical importance of tax and financial reporting. A true professional masters both. Your treasury strategy must account for your global status, and here’s what you cannot afford to ignore:

items: **Federal Taxation:** Interest earned from T-bills is subject to federal income tax at your ordinary rate. It is, however, exempt from all state and local income taxes—a significant advantage.

**Expat Reporting Requirements:** If you, as a U.S. expat, hold T-bills in a foreign brokerage account, their value counts toward your reporting thresholds for both the FBAR and Form 8938. Failure to file these can result in severe penalties.

body.content: To be crystal clear on your obligations:

rows: To prevent illicit financial activity by tracking foreign accounts.
Aggregate value of all foreign financial accounts exceeds **$10,000** at any point during the year.
To ensure tax compliance on foreign financial assets.
Total value of assets is over **$200,000** on the last day of the year or over **$300,000** at any point.

body.content: While T-bills are the gold standard for this tier, other low-risk options like Series I-Bonds or high-quality, short-term bond ETFs exist. Yet for most solo professionals, the unmatched safety, simplicity, and direct accessibility of T-bills make them the superior engine for mid-term growth.
Tier 3: The Long-Term Wealth Layer for Building Your Empire

While the safety of T-bills secures your mid-term goals, true financial autonomy requires looking much further down the road. This brings us to Tier 3, the capital you can confidently commit for at least five years, and ideally, for decades. The singular goal here is **tax-advantaged growth** to build life-changing wealth, moving beyond cash management and into strategic wealth accumulation.

As the CEO of your "Business-of-One," you have a financial superpower unavailable to traditional employees: access to high-powered retirement accounts. The **SEP IRA** and **Solo 401(k)** are the most effective tools for dramatically reducing your current tax bill while aggressively funding your future. Contribution limits for these plans are substantial, often exceeding $60,000 per year depending on your compensation.

items: A **SEP IRA** is simple to set up and allows for significant employer-only contributions (from your business to you), typically up to 25% of your compensation.

A **Solo 401(k)** offers more flexibility, allowing you to contribute as both the "employee" and the "employer." This structure can enable you to save a higher percentage of your income and may include a Roth (post-tax) option.

body.content: The strategy for this tier is defined by two principles: **automation and diversification.** The engine for your long-term growth should be a portfolio of low-cost, broadly diversified index funds or ETFs that track major market indices like the S&P 500. This approach removes the ego and emotion of trying to pick individual winners; instead, you systematically invest in the long-term growth of the entire market. You set up automatic, recurring contributions and let the power of compound growth do the heavy lifting.

This is where the entire three-tier framework demonstrates its power. By creating these distinct layers, you can confidently watch your Tier 3 portfolio weather inevitable market downturns without panic, knowing your operational lifeblood in Tier 1 and your mid-term goals in Tier 2 are completely secure. This robust structure is the hallmark of a professional operator, providing the stability to focus on building your empire.
Frequently Asked Questions
This framework gives you control, but specific questions are natural. Here are answers to the most common ones for global professionals.

items:
**What is the best alternative to Mercury Treasury for a solopreneur?**
The best alternative isn't a single product, but the strategic framework this guide outlines. The 3-Tier Capital Framework *is* the alternative:
*   **Tier 1 (Liquidity):** An FDIC-insured, high-yield business savings account.
*   **Tier 2 (Mid-Term Growth):** U.S. Treasury Bills purchased directly via TreasuryDirect.gov, giving you access to government-backed yield without the fees or high minimums of a managed product.
*   **Tier 3 (Long-Term Wealth):** Tax-advantaged retirement accounts like a SEP IRA or Solo 401(k) invested in low-cost index funds.

**How can a solopreneur earn yield on business cash safely?**
Safety means choosing the right tool for the right job and prioritizing capital preservation for operational cash.
*   **For ultimate safety:** An FDIC-insured high-yield savings account is the gold standard for your Tier 1 capital.
*   **For very low-risk yield:** U.S. Treasury Bills (T-bills) are backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. government and are an excellent tool for your Tier 2 capital—funds you don't need for at least 6-24 months.

**Is the interest from a treasury account taxable for a US expat?**
Yes, but with an important distinction. Interest income from U.S. Treasury securities is subject to federal income tax. However, it is exempt from all state and local income taxes. As a U.S. expat, you are still required to file a U.S. federal tax return and report this interest income, regardless of where you live.

**What is the real difference between SIPC and FDIC insurance?**
This is a critical distinction. They protect different assets against different types of institutional failure.

| **Feature** | **FDIC (Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation)** | **SIPC (Securities Investor Protection Corporation)** |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **What it Protects** | Cash deposits in insured bank accounts (checking, savings, CDs). | Securities and cash held in a brokerage account (stocks, bonds, mutual funds). |
| **What Triggers It** | Bank failure. | Brokerage firm failure. |
| **Coverage Limit** | Up to **$250,000** per depositor, per insured bank. | Up to **$500,000** total per customer, including a **$250,000** limit for cash. |
| **Purpose** | To protect your cash held at a bank. | To protect your investments held at a brokerage; it **does not** protect against market losses. |

**Can I buy US Treasury Bills directly as an individual?**
**Absolutely.** You can purchase T-bills directly from the U.S. government through the TreasuryDirect website. This allows you to bypass brokerage fees and buy newly issued bills at auction. You will need a Social Security or Taxpayer Identification Number, a U.S. address, and a U.S. bank account.

**What is the minimum balance required for Mercury Treasury?**
Currently, Mercury Treasury requires a minimum balance of **$250,000**. This high threshold underscores that its solution is built for well-funded companies, not the typical "Business-of-One" run by a global professional.

body.content: From Anxious Freelancer to Confident CEO of 'Me, Inc.'

You may have started this journey looking for a product, but you are leaving with something far more valuable: a durable strategy. The **3-Tier Capital Framework** is your blueprint for moving from reactive financial anxiety to proactive, authoritative control. It is the operational manual for the CEO of 'Me, Inc.'

This is about choosing **strategy over tactics**. The specific tools you use will evolve. Interest rates will fluctuate and new platforms will emerge, but the strategic framework for allocating your capital is timeless. It provides a resilient structure that empowers you to make rational decisions regardless of market noise. You can weather downturns in your long-term portfolio with confidence, knowing your operational runway is secure and your mid-term goals are funded with low-risk assets.

Ultimately, you must build your empire on a rock-solid foundation. While Gruv does not manage your investments, our platform is purpose-built to perfect your **Tier 1 foundation**. This entire treasury strategy hinges on one non-negotiable element: predictable, reliable cash flow. You cannot confidently allocate capital to Tiers 2 and 3 if your revenue is inconsistent. By ensuring every dollar you earn is invoiced compliantly, tracked with precision, and collected predictably, we provide the stable cash flow necessary to execute this strategy. Master your revenue, and you master your future.
